title: Chapter 2: The Brain's Protective Barrage
content: Sera discovered three things on her first day as the Alpha's personal maid.

One: the Wolf King's quarters were shockingly normal. A large bed with dark sheets. A writing desk covered in territorial reports. A weapons rack that held more blades than the castle armory. No skulls, no bloodstains, no evidence of throat-ripping.

Two: Kael Thornwood, in private, was even colder than in public. He acknowledged her presence exactly once — a single glance when she entered with his morning tea — and then proceeded to ignore her for the next four hours as if she were a piece of furniture that had learned to walk.
Three: his Inner Wolf was *obsessed* with her.

"So she's just... standing there. In my room. Breathing my air. Looking at my things. Should I say something? What do normal people say to their maids? 'Hello'? 'Thank you for the tea'? No, that's weird. I'm the Alpha. I don't say thank you. I say 'Your service is adequate.' But that sounds like I'm rating her. Like a restaurant. Is that — do restaurants exist in wolf territory? FOCUS, FENRIS."
Sera kept her face carefully neutral as she dusted the bookshelf for the third time. It was the only task she could do without her hands shaking.

The voice — *Fenris*, apparently that was the Inner Wolf's name — had been chattering nonstop since dawn. An endless, one-sided stream of consciousness that ranged from tactical observations about pack politics to detailed commentary on the exact shade of grey in Sera's eyes.

"Silver. No, storm-grey. No — MOON-silver. That's what I'll call it. Moon-silver eyes. Should I write a poem? Alphas don't write poems. But TECHNICALLY I'm not the Alpha, I'm the wolf side, so the rules don't apply to me. Okay, okay: 'Her eyes are moons that I would howl at forever.' No, that's terrible. 'Her eyes are — '"
Kael's pen snapped in his hand.
Sera flinched. Kael looked down at the broken pen with mild irritation — the only emotion his face had shown all morning — and reached for another without comment.
"SEE?! She flinched! The pen scared her! Apologize! SAY YOU'RE SORRY FOR BREAKING THE PEN! No wait, then she'll think you're apologizing for something else and she'll be confused and — "
A knock on the door saved Sera from having to maintain her neutral expression any longer.
"Enter," Kael said without looking up.
The door swung open, and Lady Isolde Vane swept into the room like a storm front in silk.
Sera's spine went rigid.

Isolde was everything a noble she-wolf should be. Tall and poised, with auburn hair coiled in an elaborate arrangement and a gown the color of autumn leaves. Her hazel eyes swept the room, dismissed Sera in half a heartbeat, and fixed on Kael with the intensity of a predator who had spotted wounded prey.
"My Alpha." Isolde curtsied — perfectly, of course. "I come with urgent news."
"Speak."
Isolde's gaze flicked to Sera. Lingered. "Perhaps in private?"
Kael didn't look up from his papers. "If your news is pack business, my maid's presence is irrelevant. If it's personal business, I have no interest."
Sera watched Isolde's jaw tighten — a fraction of a second, visible only because she was looking for it — before the noblewoman's pleasant expression returned.

"Very well. My Alpha, I regret to inform you that during last night's inventory of the Moon Chamber, several sacred artifacts were found... damaged." Isolde's voice dripped with practiced sorrow. "The ceremonial chalice. The ancestor's mirror. All bearing signs of careless handling."
"And this concerns me because...?"
"Because the last person seen in the Moon Chamber before the damage was discovered..." Isolde's eyes slid to Sera. "...was your new maid."
The accusation landed in the room like a stone dropped into still water.

"Oh, COME ON," Fenris howled in Sera's mind. "She was in the servant quarters all night! I know because I CHECKED! Not in a creepy way! In a... protective Alpha way! Which is ALSO creepy! BUT LESS CREEPY THAN FRAMING HER!"
Kael's golden eyes lifted from his papers. They fixed on Sera. Cold. Assessing. "Is this true?"

Sera's throat closed. She hadn't been anywhere near the Moon Chamber. She'd spent the entire previous night curled in her narrow servant's cot, trying to process the fact that she could hear the Wolf King's inner voice ordering his wolf-half to stop composing love poetry.
"I — " she started.

"She's stammering! She's scared! LOOK AT HER. Those aren't the eyes of a guilty person — those are the eyes of someone who's been bullied her whole life and just got accused of something she DIDN'T DO. Also her hair looks really pretty today. Is it shinier than yesterday? Did she wash it? With what? Do we have nice shampoo in the servant quarters? I should requisition better shampoo — "
"Silence."
Kael's voice cut through both Sera's stammering and Fenris's mental tangent. He rose from his chair — slowly, deliberately, the way a glacier might decide to change direction.
He walked toward Sera.
Step. Step. Step.

She pressed her back against the bookshelf. Her heart hammered so hard she was certain he could hear it — and then she realized with a jolt of hysterical amusement that she could hear *his* inner voice, so maybe —

"Is she trembling? Why is she trembling? I don't want her to tremble! Well, I DO, but in a DIFFERENT context — FOCUS, FENRIS. She's innocent and Isolde is lying and I need to — no, KAEL needs to — FIX THIS."

Kael stopped directly in front of Sera. He was tall enough that she had to tilt her head back to meet his eyes. Up close, the gold in them was overwhelming — hot and liquid and completely, terrifyingly unreadable.
"The castle does not shelter the useless," Kael said, his voice dropping to something barely above a murmur. "If you cannot perform even the simplest duties without causing destruction — "
"Are you SERIOUS right now?! Is THIS the act?! You're going to HUMILIATE her in front of the woman who OBVIOUSLY framed her while I'M SCREAMING AT YOU TO — "
Sera bit the inside of her cheek so hard she tasted copper.
" — then perhaps the original sentence was more appropriate," Kael finished.
Isolde's smile was a knife wrapped in silk. "My Alpha is wise."

"OH, I'LL SHOW YOU WISE. I'LL SHOW YOU — " Fenris's mental voice had gone from hysterical to genuinely furious. "KAEL, YOU ABSOLUTE BLOCK OF ICE, IF YOU LET THIS WOMAN WIN I WILL NEVER LET YOU SLEEP AGAIN. I WILL KEEP YOU AWAKE WITH LOVE POEMS. PAGES AND PAGES OF LOVE POEMS. ABOUT HER MOON-SILVER EYES AND HER TERRIFIED LITTLE FACE AND HOW YOU'RE BEING A COMPLETE — "
"Lady Vane."
Isolde straightened. "Yes, my Alpha?"
"You will report to the stablemaster at dawn tomorrow. The horses have been neglected. You will personally oversee their care for the next fortnight."
Isolde's smile froze. "I... the stables, my Alpha?"

"The Moon Chamber requires a formal investigation before blame can be assigned. In the meantime..." Kael's golden gaze flicked to Sera's trembling form, then back to Isolde. "...the pack does not shelter idleness. All wolves contribute."
"But I am a *noble* — "
"You are a wolf of the Ironmoon Pack." Kael's voice dropped to something that made the torches flicker. "Are you refusing a direct order from your Alpha?"
The silence that followed was the kind that preceded violence. Sera could feel it in her bones — the wolf energy in the room thickening, pressing down, demanding submission.
Isolde's composure cracked. Just a hair. But enough.
"No, my Alpha." The words came out strangled. "I... am honored to serve."
She curtsied again — less perfect this time — and retreated from the room with the expression of someone who had just been asked to eat poison and smile.
The door clicked shut.

Sera was still pressed against the bookshelf. Her heart was still hammering. And Kael Thornwood — the terrifying, cold-blooded, throat-ripping Wolf King — was still standing inches away from her, his golden eyes burning into hers with an expression she couldn't read.

"THAT WAS AMAZING. DID YOU SEE HER FACE? I SAW HER FACE. Well, technically I saw it through YOU because I live inside you, but STILL. That was the most beautiful thing you've ever done and I take back everything I said about you being emotionally constipated. You're emotionally... REGULAR. With occasional moments of BRILLIANCE. Now apologize to Sera for scaring her. APOLOGIZE. RIGHT NOW. Say 'I'm sorry I implied you were useless.' SAY IT, KAEL. SAY — "
Kael's hand rose.

Sera flinched — she couldn't help it, years of conditioning were stronger than any reassurance from a voice in her head — but instead of violence, his palm landed flat against the bookshelf beside her head.
A wall-slam.
The Wolf King had just wall-slammed her.

His face was inches from hers. His breath was warm — the castle was cold, but he was warm, radiating heat like a banked fire. His golden eyes traced her features with an intensity that made her feel like prey.
"The next time someone accuses you," Kael said, his voice low and rough, "you will defend yourself. I did not spare your life so you could tremble before every noble with a grudge."
His eyes dropped to her mouth. Just for a heartbeat. Then back up.
"Understand?"

"TAIL WAGGING. TAIL WAGGING SO HARD RIGHT NOW. IS THIS A ROMANTIC MOMENT? I THINK THIS IS A ROMANTIC MOMENT. SHOULD I WRITE ANOTHER POEM? 'SHE SMELLS LIKE LAVENDER AND FEAR AND I WANT TO PROTECT HER FOREVER.' Wait, is that weird? That's weird. But the lavender part is good. Keep the lavender part."
Sera swallowed. "I... understand, Alpha."
Kael held her gaze for another endless second. Then he pushed off the bookshelf and strode back to his desk as if nothing had happened.
"Finish your duties," he said, seating himself and picking up a fresh report. "And have the kitchen send up dinner at sundown."
"Yes, Alpha."

Sera's legs carried her toward the door on autopilot. Her mind was somewhere else entirely — stuck on the heat of his body, the intensity of his gaze, and the absolutely unhinged inner monologue still chattering away behind her eyes:

"She said 'Yes, Alpha.' In that soft little voice. I'm going to think about that for the REST OF MY LIFE. Fenris, mark the calendar. Today is the day she said 'Yes, Alpha' and it sounded like MUSIC. Wolf music. Howling music. I'm going to howl about it tonight. Quietly. From inside his head. Where no one can hear."
Sera closed the door behind her, pressed her forehead against the cool stone of the corridor wall, and let out a breath she hadn't realized she'd been holding.
She was the personal maid of a man whose inner wolf was writing her love poetry.
She was being targeted by a noblewoman who would stop at nothing to destroy her.
And somewhere, somehow, the Moon Goddess was laughing.
